outcome_text: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented, including those related to experimental design, data collection and selection, data processing and data curation, bias, and the factors related to the assessment of the credibility and reliability of the AI technology being used or developed.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI scientific integrity and test, evaluation, verification, and validation (TEVV) methodology, including experimental design for AI systems, data collection and curation practices, bias identification and documentation, and credibility and reliability assessment of AI technologies.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI scientific integrity and test, evaluation, verification, and validation (TEVV) methodology, including experimental design for AI systems, data collection and curation practices, bias identification and documentation, and credibility and reliability assessment of AI technologies.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and audit methods, including fairness metric selection, bias test design, explainability documentation standards, and human oversight protocols.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-2.3 (MAP) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.655, cosine: 0.679). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.647), AG-S-006 (AG, cosine: 0.640).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.655). Shared vocabulary: [ai, bias, design, documentation, knowledge, test]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assessment, collection, credibility, curation, data, evaluation] vs WIDAI terms [audit, explainability, fairness, human, methods, metric] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.885 (P=0.871, R=0.900). R exceeds P by 0.029 — the WIDAI KSA's 22 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 36 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.679 ≈ STS 0.655 (gap 0.024). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, bias, design, documentation, knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 22.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, bias, design, documentation, knowledge, test]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assessment, collection, credibility, curation, data, evaluation]. WIDAI-only: [audit, explainability, fairness, human, methods, metric].
